name: Montsagre de Paüls y Punta del Agua - Circular through the Parque Natural dels Ports
name_original: Montsagre de Paüls y Punta del Agua - Circular por el Parque Natural dels Ports
region_intro: Jogging around Paüls offers access to diverse landscapes within the Baix Ebre comarca of Catalonia, Spain. The region is characterized by the rugged terrain of Els Ports Natural Park, featuring deep ravines, cliffs, and extensive forests. Elevated routes are accessible directly from the town via the Montsagre de Paüls mountain range, providing varied challenges for runners. Natural springs are abundant, contributing to the lush environment found along many running routes. Best jogging routes around Paüls The most popular jogging route is Montsagre de Paüls y Punta del Agua - Circular through the Parque Natural dels Ports, a 10.5 miles (16.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours to complete. This route navigates the challenging terrain of Els Ports Natural Park. Another top favourite among local runners is Mola Grossa – Punta de l'Aigua loop from Paüls, a difficult 19.4 miles (31.2 km) path. This route offers significant elevation changes and expansive views of the mountainous landscape. Local runners also love the Punta de l'Aigua – Punta de l'Aigua (1098 m) loop from Paüls, a 11.1 miles (17.8 km) trail leading through the elevated areas of Els Ports Natural Park, often completed in about 3 hours 8 minutes. Jogging around Paüls is defined by rugged mountain ranges, deep ravines, and dense forests within the Els Ports Natural Park. The network offers options for various ability levels, from challenging mountain runs to more accessible paths. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Paüls's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Paüls?
A: There are over 20 dedicated running routes around Paüls, primarily located within the challenging terrain of the Els Ports Natural Park. These routes offer diverse landscapes for an engaging outdoor experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Paüls?
A: The terrain around Paüls is predominantly rugged and mountainous, especially within the Els Ports Natural Park. You'll encounter deep ravines, cliffs, and extensive forests. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949506538/montsagre-de-pauels-y-punta-del-agua-circular-through-the-parque-natural-dels-ports" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montsagre de Paüls y Punta del Agua - Circular through the Parque Natural dels Ports&lt;/a&gt; showcase this challenging environment. For a less strenuous option, the Via Verde de la Terra Alta offers a mostly flat path through tunnels and over viaducts.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Paüls?
A: While many of the listed routes in the Els Ports Natural Park, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31472088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mola Grossa – Punta de l'Aigua loop from Paüls&lt;/a&gt;, are categorized as difficult due to significant elevation changes and rugged terrain, the region also offers more accessible options. The Via Verde de la Terra Alta, a converted railway line, provides a mostly flat and consistent surface ideal for leisurely jogs or beginners.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the running routes?
A: Jogging routes in Paüls offer stunning natural features. You'll encounter the rugged beauty of the Els Ports Natural Park, the Montsagre de Paüls mountain range, and numerous natural springs like those at Font de Sant Roc. Notable peaks include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4213957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de l'Aigua (1098 m)&lt;/a&gt; and the majestic Mont Caro. The area also features striking rocky outcrops like Roques de Benet. For those interested in water features, nearby highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4184547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ferrassó Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4219810" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molí de Sotorres and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Paüls?
A: Yes, several circular running routes are available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949506538/montsagre-de-pauels-y-punta-del-agua-circular-through-the-parque-natural-dels-ports" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montsagre de Paüls y Punta del Agua - Circular through the Parque Natural dels Ports&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10.5 miles (16.9 km) long. Another challenging circular path is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24546037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de l'Aigua – Punta de l'Aigua (1098 m) loop from Paüls&lt;/a&gt;, covering 11.1 miles (17.8 km).

Q: Is Paüls a good destination for family-friendly jogging or walks?
A: While many mountain trails are challenging, Paüls does offer options suitable for families. The Via Verde de la Terra Alta is an excellent choice, providing a mostly flat and safe path away from traffic, perfect for leisurely jogs or walks with children. The Sant Roc route, described as about an hour's walk among local wildlife, can also be a pleasant, moderate option for families.

Q: Are the running trails in Paüls dog-friendly?
A: The Els Ports Natural Park, where many of the running routes are located, generally welcomes dogs. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock. Many natural springs, such as those at Font de Sant Roc, can provide water access for your pet along the trails.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Paüls?
A: The diverse climate zones within Els Ports Natural Park mean conditions can vary. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for running. Summers can be hot, especially at lower altitudes, while higher elevations might offer cooler conditions. Winters can bring colder weather to the mountainous areas.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the running routes in Paüls?
A: Paüls itself, with its traditional layout, offers local establishments where you can find refreshments. The picnic area at Sant Roc, with its natural springs, provides a lovely spot to rest and enjoy a packed meal after a run. For specific cafe or restaurant opening hours, it's best to check locally upon arrival.

Q: What are the parking options for trailheads in Paüls?
A: Parking availability can vary depending on the specific trailhead. In Paüls, you can typically find parking within the village. For routes deeper within the Els Ports Natural Park, designated parking areas may be available at popular access points. It's recommended to check specific route details or local signage for the most convenient parking spots.

Q: Are there any permits required for running on trails in Els Ports Natural Park?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for recreational running on the established trails within Els Ports Natural Park. However, it's always good practice to respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of the natural environment. For any specific activities or large groups, it's advisable to check with the park authority.

Q: What makes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33693369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lo Tormassal – Punta de l'Aigua (1098 m) loop from Paüls&lt;/a&gt; a popular choice?
A: This 8.2-mile (13.3 km) loop is favored for its significant elevation gain of over 800 meters, offering a challenging workout and rewarding views of the mountainous landscape. It provides a direct experience of the elevated areas within Els Ports Natural Park, making it a great option for runners seeking a demanding yet scenic route.
tips: La Punta de l'Aigua is a mountain located near Paüls, in the Lower Ebro region, Catalonia. It is one of the most prominent peaks in the area and is part of the 100 Peaks challenge of the Federation of Hiking Entities of Catalonia (FEEC).

The path enters the territories of La Refoia and runs along the shore of Bassa del Coll Roig. The landscape is a striking contrast of limestone ridges emerging bare over scattered black pine forests, ravines and mountain pastures, leaving striking empty spaces that provide a pleasant state of contemplation.

At the first fork in the path, you can take a shortcut directly through a spur in the land to the path that climbs to the geodesic vertex of Tossal d'Engrilló (1,073 m), planted right on the edge of the precipices of the west face. The views are magnificent. The summit is the last southern level of the Natural Park of Els Ports that exceeds a thousand meters of altitude.
